purpose: Category A salvage — cases that are structurally unfixable under the SMILES-only admission channel because the ground-truth family is distinguished ONLY by reagent identity (not by atom/bond transformation). These cases are not thrown away; they are parked for Phase 5 (Reagent-Aware Admission Track).

remediation_plan.proposed_schema_change: Add `reagent_canonical_smiles TEXT` column to extract_molecules (or a sibling extract_reagents table) and populate from DB reagent_text fields
remediation_plan.proposed_predictor_change: Extend evidence_search feature extraction to consume reagent SMILES; add reagent-anchored guards (DMSO/oxalyl chloride → Swern; IBX/periodinane → Dess-Martin; salen-Mn → Jacobsen; fructose-ketone → Shi; etc.)
remediation_plan.proposed_benchmark_change: Introduce a second admission benchmark layer `family_admission_benchmark_with_reagents.json` keyed on the same case_ids
remediation_plan.success_criterion: ≥6/9 of these cases admitted at top-1 once reagent channel is live; zero regression on the 259-case SMILES-only admission baseline

safety_note: These cases remain in the main admission benchmark with `disallow_top3=true` for their current greedy confusers. They will continue to miss under SMILES-only — this is expected and not a regression.

db_provenance_audit_2026_04_24.finding: All 8 case record_ids map to `reactions` rows whose source_summary is a Korean organic-chemistry textbook page header (e.g., "맥머리유기화학_19장#p10"). record_type is `text_fragment`/`reaction_text` with is_structure_indexable=0 and reaction_smiles=NULL. Joined reaction_cards and reaction_conditions return EMPTY STRINGS for reagents/solvent/conditions; notes carries only section-heading prose. reaction_extracts.scheme_candidate_id with matching IDs is not semantically aligned (843→Aza-Wittig, 871→Table-of-Contents).
db_provenance_audit_2026_04_24.implication_for_phase5: DB has no reagent text to backfill. Phase 5 (#120) reagent channel cannot source from reaction_cards/reaction_conditions for these rows. Options: (a) harvest reagent text from the textbook page image corresponding to source_summary via Gemini vision OCR; (b) synthesize reagent SMILES directly from `canonical_reagent_fingerprint` (already documented per-case below) using a hand-curated reagent ontology; (c) treat this 8-case cohort as a synthesis target and accept that the feature source is the ontology, not the ingest pipeline.
db_provenance_audit_2026_04_24.recommended_path_for_phase5: Option (b) — curate canonical reagent SMILES directly from the fingerprint strings already present in this file. This is cheaper than page OCR and deterministic. Use the ontology as the source of truth; DB ingest pipeline stays untouched.

cases.why_structurally_unfixable: Swern/Corey-Kim/Pfitzner-Moffatt/Dess-Martin/Ley all convert ROH → RCHO with no distinguishing atom count / bond change. SMILES-only input carries zero discriminating signal.
Alcohol → aldehyde with no structural tell.
Alcohol → aldehyde with no structural tell.
Alcohol → aldehyde with no structural tell.
Jacobsen/Shi/Prilezhaev all give styrene → styrene oxide. No stereochemistry in the benchmark SMILES to distinguish chiral variants.
Same substrate/product as Jacobsen/Shi — reagent (mCPBA / peracid) is the only tell.
Schlosser vs classical Wittig differs by base (PhLi at low T → E-selective). Benchmark SMILES contains no stereo; indistinguishable from classical Wittig without reagent.

Ketone → alkene with one fewer degree of unsaturation; same transformation achievable via Wolff-Kishner (giving saturated), Schwartz (distinct), etc. Differentiation requires the TsNHNH2 / RLi reagent signature.
cases.co_flag_see_also: Originally considered for Category C (defect) — ruled OUT because Shapiro mechanism legitimately yields the less-substituted alkene (1-hexene from 2-hexanone), which is chemically sound.
